    My Account

    News

    Mayor of Richmond upon Thames invites residents to charity launch reception

    Residents are invited to join the Mayor of Richmond upon Thames, Councillor Nancy Baldwin, for a special charity launch reception at the historic Asgill House on Richmond Riverside on Sunday 5 July 2026. 

    Council news | 30 Jun 26

    Become a London Heartstarter in Kew

    The London Heart Starters campaign is bringing more life-saving defibrillators to the city, in the areas where they are needed most. Join Rotary Club of Kew Gardens and London Ambulance Service on 6 August to learn how to save a life.

    Community news | Rotary Club of Kew Gardens | 30 Jun 26

    Catch Shakespeare's Merry Wives in York House Gardens

    From 13-18 July, the Richmond Shakespeare Society's annual open-air show returns to York House Gardens.

    Community news | Richmond Shakespeare Society | 30 Jun 26

    Don't risk it. Don't jump into the Thames at Teddington Lock

    As great as it can be for cooling off, it is important to remember that the Thames is a dangerous river - cold water shock, shallow water and debris can all cause serious risk to life.

    Council news | 29 Jun 26

    Care for wildlife while you enjoy the parks

    As the warm weather arrives, our parks become the perfect place for a walk, a picnic, and to enjoy nature. These spaces are special because we share them with wildlife, and protecting them starts with small thoughtful choices. 

    Council news | 26 Jun 26

    Better connected: New strategy starts journey to improved transport links for Richmond

    A bold new strategy to improve mobility throughout Richmond upon Thames by strengthening the borough’s transport networks has been launched by the Council.

    Council news | 25 Jun 26

    Summer of suspense at Twickenham Library

    Whether you’re already a fan of gripping crime fiction or simply looking for your next great read, join us for an exciting evening with four outstanding writers on Thursday 9 July at Twickenham Library.

    Council news | 25 Jun 26

    Live Languages: Charity French coffee morning at ETNA

    Calling all Francophiles! On Saturday 5 September, residents of all ages and levels of French are invited to practise their French conversation at ETNA Community Centre.

    Community news | Live Languages | 25 Jun 26

    Barnes mum builds village to end family loneliness

    Loneliness doesn't care if you live somewhere beautiful. Parents are standing next to each other in Barnes, on the Common, at the school gate - and they are completely alone. Chloe Stephens, a single mum with nearly 30 years in childcare, built The Village Barnes to change that.

    Community news | The Village Barnes | 25 Jun 26

    Courage at the heart of Richmond’s Refugee Week celebrations

    In Richmond, we take our role as a Borough of Sanctuary seriously and are committed to creating a unified community by bringing our sanctuary seekers and residents together. Creating meaningful social connections, cross cultural conversations and celebrating our diversity is what builds cohesion and integration. And that is exactly what happened during recent Refugee Week.

    Council blog | 25 Jun 26

    Free webinar – Ask the experts: solar panels and retrofitting in Richmond

    Thinking about solar panels, batteries, or planning wider energy upgrades to your home? We are hosting a free 'ask me anything' webinar to help you get started.

    Council news | 24 Jun 26

    Forest e-bike rollout progressing well as Lime service ends 26 June

    The rollout of Forest e-bikes across Richmond upon Thames is well underway, with Lime’s service now in the final phase of the transition ahead of full changeover on Friday 26 June.

    Council news | 24 Jun 26

    Apply for free space to grow your art project

    Our Artists Make Space programme supports artists by providing free studio space across the borough. Submit your application by Sunday 5 July.

    Council news | 24 Jun 26

    Centre Stage: Richmond Myths programme revealed as countdown begins to summer festival weekend

    With just over four weeks until Richmond’s biggest free cultural weekend of the summer, the full programme for Centre Stage: Richmond Myths has been unveiled.

    Council news | 24 Jun 26

    Young Eco Champions empowered to spark climate action across Richmond upon Thames

    45 primary school pupils from 8 local schools across Richmond upon Thames came together this week for an inspiring Eco Summit designed to empower young people to take action for nature and the climate.

    Council news | 24 Jun 26

    Climate action in Richmond during Climate Action Week 2026

    It’s Climate Action Week, and we’re sharing how we’re taking action on climate change across Richmond.

    Council news | 23 Jun 26

    Civic Pride Fund empowers community effort to save Barnes’ swifts

    Our Civic Pride Fund recently supported a powerful community led conservation project by local charity Barnes Common Limited.

    Council news | 23 Jun 26

    The power of partnership: Community BlueScapes Fair celebrates flood resilience and climate action

    Residents, families, partners, and stakeholders flocked to the OSO Arts Centre in Barnes on Saturday for the latest Community BlueScapes Fair.

    Council news | 23 Jun 26

    Celebrate and support our local small charities

    Running from 22 to 29 June, Small Charity Week is a chance to celebrate the incredible contribution of local charities that make Richmond upon Thames a compassionate and connected borough.

    Council news | 22 Jun 26

    Innovative flood-resilience project gains national recognition as finalist for multiple awards

    Richmond’s first flood resilient street has achieved more recognition for its pioneering approach. 

    Council news | 22 Jun 26

    Special offer: 100 weddings to celebrate 100 years of York House

    2026 marks the centenary of York House as a public building and to celebrate Richmond Council is offering 100 couples the opportunity to be part of this historic milestone.

    Council news | 22 Jun 26

    Record investment programme drives next phase of Petersham Road upgrades

    Improvement works continue on Petersham Road next month, as part of our ambitious £21 million investment programme to upgrade roads and pavements across the borough.

    Council news | 19 Jun 26

    Richmond celebrates culture and courage during Refugee Week

    Local groups, residents and sanctuary seekers came together for a vibrant community celebration for Refugee Week 2026. With World Refugee Day on 20 June, the event highlighted the moments that brought our community together.

    Council news | 19 Jun 26

    Heathrow expansion consultation launched on Clean Air Day ‘deserves fresh criticism’, says Richmond Council Leader

    Richmond Council’s Leader has today criticised the government's decision to launch a consultation on Heathrow expansion on Clean Air Day – calling the timing ‘ironic’ – and warning that a third runway would mean more noise, more pollution and more disruption for communities already affected by the airport.

    Council news | 18 Jun 26

    Play tennis in Richmond this summer

    Inspired by Wimbledon to dust of your racket or learn to play tennis? There are 11 public tennis court sites to choose from in Richmond upon Thames.

    Council news | 18 Jun 26

    Teddington School celebrates achieving Gold Clean Air School Accreditation status

    Teddington School has become the first in the borough to receive a new clean air accreditation, with it achieving gold status.

    Council news | 18 Jun 26

    Ready to give your walks a purpose? Try the newly-launched Box Walks

    Box Walks is a new initiative using cleaned-up old phone boxes for a nature walk, with information at each phone box about a different aspect of climate change and sustainability.

    Community news | Box Walks | 18 Jun 26

    Bubble show aims to bring joy across generations and raise money for charity

    Local award-winning charity, Brighter Together, have been selected to participate in The Big Give Small Charity Week from 22 to 29 June, where all public donations will be match funded.

    Community news | Brighter Together | 18 Jun 26

    Richmond’s history as a welcoming Borough of Sanctuary on display

    A new information board has been installed at the Garden of Sanctuary, helping visitors learn more about the borough’s longstanding commitment to supporting people seeking sanctuary.

    Council news | 17 Jun 26

    Richmond employers unite to boost opportunities for young people

    Leading employers from across Richmond upon Thames have come together to explore how new national apprenticeship reforms are changing the way businesses invest in skills – and how more young people could access training and employment opportunities locally.

    Council news | 16 Jun 26

    Richmond Sustainability Forum brings repairs and reuse to life at The Fixery

    More than 200 people gathered at Grove Gardens on Saturday to attend the eighth Richmond Sustainability Forum and explore The Fixery – Richmond’s new repair and reuse hub in the recently refurbished Grove Gardens Chapel.

    Council news | 16 Jun 26

    Join Richmond Mencap's Learning Disability Awareness Day event

    Join Richmond Mencap at York House on 17 June for a fantastic community celebration packed with fun, food, and fantastic entertainment. Browse exciting stalls, enjoy delicious food, and soak up live music performances throughout the day.

    Community news | Richmond Mencap | 16 Jun 26

    New vouchers help residents and businesses try cargo bikes for less

    Residents and businesses in Richmond upon Thames are being invited to try a cleaner, greener way to travel, with our latest cargo bike offers.

    Council news | 11 Jun 26

    Home-Start celebrating 30 years of local impact

    For 30 years, Home-Start Richmond Kingston and Hounslow has supported families across Richmond, Kingston and Hounslow facing challenges like isolation, mental health difficulties, disability, financial issues and domestic abuse.

    Community news | Home-Start Richmond Kingston and Hounslow | 11 Jun 26

    Soar for Skylarks over the Allianz

    Get ready for the ultimate adrenaline rush! Join us for the Twickenham Zipwire, an unforgettable 150m zipline adventure across the Allianz Stadium, the home of English rugby. Feel the thrill as you soar high above the pitch while friends, family, and fellow participants cheer you on.

    Community news | Skylarks Charity | 10 Jun 26

    Armed Forces Week: Open day at The Poppy Factory

    The Poppy Factory will hold a free open day at the start of Armed Forces Week on Monday 22 June, as the charity celebrates 100 years in Richmond upon Thames.

    Community news | The Poppy Factory | 10 Jun 26

    Give your items a second life at this month's Twickenham Repair Café

    The next Twickenham Repair Café will be open on Saturday 20 June, for the repair of household items.

    Community news | Twickenham Repair Café | 10 Jun 26

    Elleray Social Club: Forming new friendships later in life

    Growing older can bring more time to relax, fewer daily pressures and the chance to spend more time enjoying hobbies and family. Yet for some, it also brings a quieter, less welcome visitor – social isolation and loneliness.

    Community news | The Elleray Centre and Elleray Social Club | 10 Jun 26

    Residents invited to attend the Safer Neighbourhood Board meeting this July

    Residents are invited to attend the next Richmond upon Thames Safer Neighbourhood Board (SNB) meeting, taking place at York House in Twickenham on Thursday 9 July at 7pm.

    Community news | Richmond Safer Neighbourhood Board | 10 Jun 26

    Thames Path 30th Anniversary Relay

    In celebration of the Thames Path’s 30th anniversary, and in collaboration with Drinkable Thames, a month long relay is taking place from the source in the Cotswolds to Woolwich, passing through Richmond.

    Community news | Thames Landscape Strategy | 10 Jun 26

    Building a carer-friendly community in Richmond

    From Monday 8 to Sunday 14 June, Carers Week shines a spotlight on the vital contribution of unpaid carers across our community and highlights the important of 'Building Carer Friendly Communities'.

    Council news | 09 Jun 26

    Community‑led cargo bike project takes off in the borough

    This Bike Week (8 to 14 June), hear from our team at Richmond Cargo Collective. Discover how local parents are transforming their school run and our streets by swapping cars for cargo bikes and inspiring other families to do the same.

    Partner blog by Richmond Cargo Collective | 08 Jun 26

    New wetlands for a greener borough

    In Richmond, we’re fortunate to have the River Thames winding through our borough, shaping the places where people walk, relax and connect with nature.

    Council news | 05 Jun 26

    Community power: caring for our trees

    National environmental charity The Tree Council brought more than 30 volunteers from nine organisations to Kneller Gardens in Twickenham for a day of community giveback. Discover what they achieved and explore simple ways you can get involved. 

    Council news | 04 Jun 26

    Community progress in bringing back one of London's rarest plants

    Earlier this year, we joined a London-wide collaborative conservation programme bringing together people and organisations across London to protect and restore tower mustard (Turritis glabra), one of the UK’s rarest native plants.

    Council news | 04 Jun 26

    Design a poster to celebrate 150 years of St Margarets Station

    Get creative and help celebrate the 150th birthday of St Margarets Station with a colourful poster design.

    Community news | Visit Richmond | 04 Jun 26

    Enjoy the Museum of Richmond's Trumpeters' House Garden Party

    Unwind in a lovely garden with your favourite drink and a delicious slice of cake, all while enjoying the uplifting sounds of the Barnes Concert Band and fundraising for the Museum of Richmond.

    Community news | Museum of Richmond | 04 Jun 26

    RSS Young Actors' Summer Showcase

    As part of the 2026 RSS ALIVE! programme, the RSS Young Actors' Company presents its Summer Showcase, featuring some of the work they have been preparing this year.

    Community news | Richmond Shakespeare Society | 04 Jun 26

    The Exchange announces Cinderella as 2026 panto

    The Exchange in Twickenham has announced Cinderella as its pantomime for 2026 opening on Monday 7 December for a 20-day run, the longest ever running pantomime production at the venue.

    Community news | The Exchange | 04 Jun 26

    Artist of the Year Competition

    The Arts Richmond Artist of the Year Competition will take place on Saturday 20 June 2026 at Ham House.

    Community news | Arts Richmond | 04 Jun 26

    Twickenham Riverside transformation gathers pace as major construction begins

    The transformation of Twickenham Riverside is moving rapidly ahead, with major construction works now underway across the site as the long-awaited regeneration of the town’s waterfront takes shape.

    Council news | 03 Jun 26

    Shining a light on Richmond’s volunteer community

    This Volunteers’ Week (1 to 7 June), Richmond CVS is sharing insights from their recent Volunteer Fair. Read on for their reflections and to discover how you can get involved. 

    Partner blog by Richmond CVS | 02 Jun 26

    Celebrate pride and love across the borough

    This June, Pride Month is lighting up Richmond upon Thames with nature adventures, creative workshops, community meetups and book displays celebrating LGBTQ+ voices.

    Council news | 01 Jun 26

    Celebrate courage this Refugee Week

    From 15 to 21 June, Refugee Week 2026 invites everyone to reflect on the theme of ‘Courage’. Come along and experience a programme of inspiring events celebrating the resilience, creativity and diversity of sanctuary seekers in our borough. 

    Council news | 01 Jun 26

    Forest launches across Richmond upon Thames today – delivering cheaper fares, more bikes and a better deal for residents

    A new era for e-bike travel begins today in Richmond upon Thames as Forest officially launches its borough-wide hire e-bike service, bringing lower prices, more bikes, and better connectivity for residents.

    Council news | 01 Jun 26

    Family bike ride and day of fun at Osterley Park

    Join fellow Richmond families on Saturday 13 June for a lovely family bike ride from York House, Twickenham to the beautiful Osterley Park, where you can enjoy the West London Cycling Mela.

    Community news | Richmond Cargo Collective | 01 Jun 26
